arts.ttl: Ruben Selles hails 'massive' Hull City win over Preston to boost survival hopes
arts.sum: Ruben Selles praised the comeback which saw Hull City roar back from a goal down at half-time to beat Preston North End 2-1 and give their hopes of survival a major boost. Gelhardt made no mistake and sent the goalkeeper the wrong way to lift the roof off the MKM Stadium, and in truth, bar a couple of nervy moments, City eased a victory that keeps them two points above the drop zone with two games to play.

arts.ttl: Championship roundup: Gelhardt’s spot-kicks give Hull crucial win over Preston
arts.sum: Hull beat Preston 2-1 as four of bottom five win Millwall bolster playoff hopes as Coventry and Boro falter Joe Gelhardt scored two second-half penalties as Hull boosted their Championship survival hopes by coming from behind to beat their fellow strugglers Preston 2-1. Lewis Gibson put the visitors ahead to give them hope of ending a five-match winless run that has dragged them into trouble, but Gelhardt twice struck from the spot at a rainy MKM Stadium. Defeat for Hull would have put them in the bottom three after Derby and Luton also won, but Rubén Sellés’ side remain two points above the drop zone in 20th. They can guarantee their Championship status by beating Derby at the weekend, while a third successive loss leaves Preston three points above 22nd-placed Luton and desperately low on confidence. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Joe Gelhardt the hero as Hull City stage stunning turnaround to beat Preston North End
arts.sum: Selles made four changes from the side beaten at Swansea on Friday, with Steven Alzate back in the side at the expense of Gustavo Puerta, while Nordin Amrabat was handed a first start in place of Lincoln, and there were returns to the starting XI for Abu Kamara and Kyle Joseph in place of Kasey Palmer and Joao Pedro. City turned things on their head with just over 20 minutes to go, when McLoughlin's shot was beaten away by Cornell, but Joseph was quick to the rebound and was sent to the floor, referee Bramall pointed to the spot for a second time.

arts.ttl: Hull City player ratings vs Preston North End as Nordin Amrabat earns standing ovation
arts.sum: The Tigers pulled level at the start of the second half when Joe Gelhardt was fouled, and stepped up to slot in the penalty and he had another chance, when Kyle Joseph was sent tumbling, sending goalkeeper David Cornell the wrong way to turn the game on its head, and they saw it out to claim a big home win. City were a goal down just after the half-hour when Lewis Gibson turned in from close range after the Tigers gifted a needless corner, and were unable to clear.

arts.ttl: Joe Gelhardt’s penalty double earns Hull a vital victory against Preston
arts.sum: Ruben Selles’ side remain two points above the drop zone in 20th.

arts.ttl: Throw a bomb under the lot and start again'- Paul Heckingbottom blast ahead of Hull City clash
arts.sum: That’s got to be what drives you and makes sure you make the changes," Heckingbottom blasted. That’s what I’d do, but I don’t think I’ll be able to do that, so it’s going to be hard work.

arts.ttl: Frustrated Hull City chief calls for chaos as Championship status in balance
arts.sum: Ruben Selles wants to see his side create some chaos in the final third as he looks to mastermind a much-needed win on Easter Monday. "Well, we are not going to make something magical in that time, it's not going to change a lot," Selles said when asked what he can do to try and find a spark in the final third.

arts.ttl: Hull City inquest into woeful season must wait as relegation battle bites hard
arts.sum: Following a thrilling 3-3 draw with Ipswich Town in their final home outing, Rosenior and owner Acun Ilicali took the applause from a fan base reinvigorated by a season which saw City challenge at the top end of the Championship for the first time since they won promotion to Premier League back in 2016 via that iconic play-off final win at Wembley. Few could argue that things at Hull City have unravelled spectacularly since the sacking of Liam Rosenior just days after last season ended with a 1-0 defeat at Plymouth Argyle.
